name: Curses and Magical Contagions
description: Rules for curses, blessed conditions that burden creatures, and magical contagions that spread among populations

curses.title: Curses
curses.description: A curse is a magical burden that lasts for a specified time or until ended by some means
curses.overview: Every curse has rules that govern it. A curse typically takes one of the forms detailed below.

bestow_curse.name: Bestow Curse
bestow_curse.description: The simplest curses are created by the Bestow Curse spell
details: Effects of such curses are limited and can be ended by the Remove Curse spell
Provides useful benchmarks for gauging the potency of other curses
A curse that lasts for 1 minute equates to a level 3 spell
A curse that lasts until dispelled equates to a level 9 spell

cursed_creatures.name: Cursed Creatures
cursed_creatures.description: Some monsters are associated with curses, whether as part of their origins or due to their ability to spread curses
cursed_creatures.dm_guidance: You decide how a spell like Remove Curse affects a creature with accursed origins. For example, you might decide that a mummy was created through a curse and it can be destroyed permanently only by casting Remove Curse on its corpse.

cursed_magic_items.name: Cursed Magic Items
cursed_magic_items.description: Cursed magic items are created deliberately or originate as the result of supernatural events
cursed_magic_items.note: Such items are detailed in Magic Items section

narrative_curses.name: Narrative Curses
narrative_curses.description: A curse that manifests during an adventure when a creature's violation of a taboo warrants supernatural punishment
characteristics: Can have any effects the DM designs
Might be a customized version of another type of curse
Affected creature should know why they're being punished
Creature should be able to learn how to end their curse by symbolically righting the wrong they committed
mechanics: How a spell like Remove Curse affects such a curse is up to the DM
The spell might merely suppress the effects of the curse for a time
Narrative curses should feel like rare, potent magic rooted in the lore of your campaign

environmental_curses.name: Environmental Curses
environmental_curses.description: Some locations are so suffused with evil that anyone who lingers there is burdened with a curse

demonic_possession.title: Demonic Possession
demonic_possession.description: Demonic Possession arises from the chaos and evil of the Abyss
demonic_possession.origin: Commonly besets creatures that interact with demonic objects or linger in desecrated locations, where demonic spirits await victims

infection.trigger: A creature becomes the target of Demonic Possession
infection.effect: On a failed save, the creature is possessed by a bodiless demonic entity

control.trigger: Whenever the possessed creature rolls a 1 on a D20 Test
control.effect: The demonic entity takes control of the creature and determines the creature's behavior

recovery.trigger: At the end of each of the possessed creature's later turns
recovery.effect: The creature regains control of itself on a success

long_rest_cure.effect: On a successful save, the effect ends on the creature

removal: Any magic that removes a curse

magical_contagions.title: Magical Contagions
magical_contagions.description: An adverse effect of magical origin that is contagious by definition
magical_contagions.adventure_use: An outbreak of such a contagion can form the basis of an adventure as characters search for a cure and try to stop the contagion's spread

rest_and_recuperation.title: Rest and Recuperation
rest_and_recuperation.description: If a creature infected with a magical contagion spends 3 days recuperating, engaging in no activities that would interrupt a Long Rest

mechanics.trigger: At the end of the recuperation period
mechanics.effect_on_success: The creature has Advantage on saving throws to fight off the magical contagion for the next 24 hours

cackle_fever.name: Cackle Fever
cackle_fever.source: Cheaply made potions and elixirs that are sometimes tainted

fever.title: Fever
fever.effect: The creature gains 1 Exhaustion level, which lasts until the contagion ends on the creature

uncontrollable_laughter.title: Uncontrollable Laughter
uncontrollable_laughter.trigger: While the creature has the Exhaustion condition
mechanics: Each time the creature takes damage other than Psychic damage, it makes a DC 13 Constitution saving throw
On a failed save, the creature takes 5 (1d10) Psychic damage and has the Incapacitated condition as it laughs uncontrollably
At the end of each of its turns, the creature repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success

fighting_the_contagion.frequency: At the end of each Long Rest
fighting_the_contagion.cure: After the creature succeeds on three of these saves, the contagion ends on it, and the creature is immune to Cackle Fever for 1 year

spreading.trigger: Any Humanoid (other than a gnome) that starts its turn within a 10-foot Emanation originating from an infected creature
spreading.effect: On a failed save, the Humanoid also becomes infected with the contagion
spreading.immunity: On a successful save, the Humanoid can't catch the contagion from that particular infected creature for the next 24 hours

sewer_plague.name: Sewer Plague
sewer_plague.environment: Incubates in sewers and refuse heaps
vectors: Transmission by creatures that dwell in such areas, including otyughs and rats
Any Humanoid that is wounded by a creature that carries the contagion
Contact with contaminated filth or offal

weakness.title: Weakness
weakness.trigger: While the creature has any Exhaustion levels
weakness.effect: It regains only half the normal number of Hit Points from spending Hit Point Dice

restlessness.title: Restlessness
restlessness.trigger: While the creature has any Exhaustion levels
restlessness.effect: Finishing a Long Rest neither restores lost Hit Points nor reduces the creature's Exhaustion level

mechanics: On a failed save, the creature gains 1 Exhaustion level as its fatigue worsens
On a successful save, the creature's Exhaustion level decreases by 1
If the creature's Exhaustion level is reduced to 0, the contagion ends on the creature

sight_rot.name: Sight Rot
sight_rot.transmission: Drinks water tainted by Sight Rot
sight_rot.effect_on_failure: Have the Blinded condition until the contagion ends

nonmagical_treatment.result: Create one dose of nonmagical ointment (1 hour to create)
nonmagical_treatment.application: When applied to the eyes of a creature suffering from Sight Rot, the ointment suppresses the contagion on that creature for 24 hours
nonmagical_treatment.cure: If the contagion is suppressed in this way for a total of 72 hours (requiring three doses and applications of the ointment), the contagion ends on the creature

spreading.trigger: Any Humanoid that makes skin contact with a creature infected with Sight Rot
spreading.effect: On a failed save, the Humanoid also becomes infected with the contagion
spreading.immunity: On a successful save, the Humanoid can't catch the contagion from that particular infected creature for the next 24 hours
